ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Peterson (Trustee) appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Markey Quayle appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the November 30, 2020 order reads that the entire corpus of the trust was to be distributed to the Beneficiaries and stated that it is unclear as to why there are funds still remaining in this trust. Mr. Peterson stated that this is specifically because of disputes that took over a year, that there was substantial income during this time and that he felt it was appropriate to have an accounting of the income during that year. Ms. Quayle stated that she and her Brothers felt that the trust should have been distributed a long time ago, that there is an issue of a diving helmet for which she offered to pay for it over a year ago, that the helmet was valued at $10,000.00 and that Peter (Brother) has it. Ms. Quayle also stated that Mr. Peterson is charging attorney fees, that it is long past their need for his services, that she hired Chandler Property Management property to manage the property, that she does not understand why Mr. Peterson collected the utilities and rents, and that Mr. Peterson billed for services long after November. In addition, Ms. Quayle stated that Mr. Peterson is claiming that the delay was because of filing of the petition and that she does not understand why Mr. Peterson would have to prepare the tax return for the trust. The Court gave Ms. Quayle the option of continuing the matter for filing of her objections in writing to show what attorney's fees she is contesting, or for the Court to go on what she has said today. Ms. Quayle elected for the Court to go on what she has said, stated that she would like to be reimbursed for the payment she provided for her Mother's care, and that she would like the remaining cash on hand to be distributed to her. Mr. Peterson stated that the last accounting ended on December 31, 2019, that there was one full year of income, that he did not charge anything for property management as of the time Chandler took over, that the reason he had to file this petition was because three of the Beneficiaries did not sign the receipts and that this has been delayed by the Beneficiaries. Mr. Peterson also stated that Judge Moody approved $250.00 per hour for trustee's fees, that he had to file the income taxes for the last year and that it if he didn't file it, nobody would have filed it. The Court stated that the Beneficiaries are to work out the issue of the diving helmet amongst themselves. Mr. Peterson stated that everyone has signed a receipt for one quarter of the value of the diving helmet and that they need to work it out themselves. The Seventh and Final Account and Report of Successor Trustee Robert H. Peterson, III, Petition for Its Settlement, Petition for Allowance of Trustee Fees, and Petition for Distribution is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-17-2020 Continued To Sep-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Peterson appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Marky Quayle in Propria Persona.
Peter Quayle appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Jeffrey Quayle appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Dakota Quayle appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Mr. Peterson stated that they are still having a dispute over the missing carved chest and the ship model, that he believes Marky took those items, that they are worth $650.00 and that the real property is worth $8,000,000.00. The Court noted that this trust is being held up by a $650.00 dispute and raised the issue of having a deposition. Mr. Peterson stated that he requested taking Marky's deposition and that if Marky would sign a declaration under penalty and perjury stating that she does not have the items, he will take the $650.00 out of his fees and give it to Jeffrey. Peter, Jeffrey and Dakota all stated that they are not in a hurry to sell the real property. The parties raised issues that are not before the Court (rolling desk, table and chairs, the loan to Peter) and therefore, the Court did not address them. Discussion was held as to how to dissolve the trust, whether or not the parties would consider mediation, distributing the real property in shares to each of the Siblings, the difficulty in getting a loan in the name of a trust, whether some assurance can be provided to Marky for payment of the $335,000.00 loan, whether it is appropriate for the trust to pay for Marky's caregiver fees that were incurred in the conservatorship, the possibility of a lien, having a settlement agreement, etc. The Court wants the parties to meet and confer, and encourages the parties to come up with a plan. The Sixth and Final Report and Account of Trustee, etc. is continued to September 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status statement addressing the parties positions as to dissolving of the trust and Marky's debts is to be filed by September 21, 2020.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2020 Continued To Aug-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Peterson (Trustee) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Marky Quayle appeared in Propria Persona.
Dakota Quayle appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Jeffrey Quayle appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Peter Quayle appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted the value of the items each party was to receive, stated that if Mr. Peterson allowed the parties to do something, he did not supervise it and that there is a significant dispute about whether or not the personal property was received, or not. Mr. Peterson stated that a surcharge on the Trustee is unfair, that he left it up to the three children to divide the items amongst themselves, that they did it by drawing straws and that the children then communicated to him who got what. Mr. Peterson also stated that Jeffrey had every opportunity to take his items, that if Jeffrey failed to do so it's on Jeffrey, that Jeffrey left behind his items in Phyllis Quayle's unit, that they had to fix it up to get it rented and that he gave Marky permission to remove the items. In addition, Mr. Peterson stated that he believes Marky kept the items and that he does not believe Marky took the items to the dumps because they were valuable items and antiques. Marky spoke of the $100,000.00 she loaned to Peter for the purchase of a mobile home, stated that Peter loaded up his vehicle and said that he was going to come back, that Peter did not come back and that Mr. Peterson ordered her to post a note indicating that if the items were not removed by a certain date, they would be taken to the dumps. Discussion was held regarding family dynamics, descriptions of the personal property, the process of drawing straws not being done unfairly, Marky having received 90% of the items, the items they did not receive, the children not believing that the items were taken to the dumps, the children not agreeing with a lien against the trust for the loan to Jeffrey, not agreeing that Marky could up with something that they can all agree on, not feeling that owning the property with Marky will work out, the children not wanting the property to be sold until after the corona virus is over, etc. In response to Marky's suggestion to have Mr. Peterson distribute the trust to the four children and having Chandler Property Management pay her $25,000.00 a month out of the trust for money that is owed to her, Mr. Peterson stated that the $100,000.00 was personal loan to Jeffrey and that the $73,000.00 has to do with the conservatorship. Marky objects to Mr. Peterson charging Attorney rates. In support of his fee request, Mr. Peterson stated that this includes the 5th and 6th account and report, spoke of the various petitions he had to file, stated that he believes the fees are reasonable, and noted that there are no separate attorney's fees being charged and that he has saved money to the trust. The Court stated that from a legal point of view the personal items are listed on the inventory and appraisal, and if someone is claiming that they did not receive them, there is the prospect of a surcharge. The Sixth and Final Report and Account of Trustee, etc. is continued to August 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 so that the parties can talk about how to handle Marky's loan and to review an appropriate amount of bond to address the potential issue of a surcharge for the personal items. Mr. Peterson is to make sure Dakota is properly noticed.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions Re Distribution To Beneficiary Under Age 40 And For Distribution Of Personal Property
Off Calendar Aug-13-2019 Continued To Oct-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Robert Howard Peterson III (Trustee). Christopher Rillo for and with Markey L. Quayle. Peter Quayle was also present.

The Court addressed two issues: 1) whether the age 40 provision in the trust regarding children applies to grandchildren and 2) the personal property. The Court addressed Peter Quayle inquiring about his position/aligning with regard to Dakota and the age 40 provision. Mr. Quayle stated that he agrees that Dakota should not have any of the money until she reaches age 40. Mr. Rillo stated that they can submit additional briefing, that Mr. Quayle needs to contact the appraiser, that there needs to be an updated appraisal, and that there is art/paintings that need to be appraised. Mr. Peterson stated that the personal property is stored in a basement, that the basement is occupied by Peter Quayle, that the appraisal needs to be done, and that the appraiser (James Snidle) will need access to the basement. Brief discussion regarding a specific date for the appraisal are held. Mr. Quayle stated that the appraisal can be done on Friday, August 23, 2019. The Court then bifurcated the petition and continued the bifurcated issue of the personal property to October 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that access to the property is to be given to the appraiser on August 23, 2019 or on the first agreed upon date by the appraiser and parties. The Court then addressed the bifurcated issue of the age 40 provision. Each counsel presented argument in support of their respective positions with regard to the age 40 provision. At the conclusion of argument, the Court took the bifurcated issue of the age 40 provision under submission. Each side is to prepare a proposed order and the orders should include language allowing distribution now or with distribution at age 40. Also, Mr. Rillo's order should include language regarding the fees being reimbursed and Mr. Peterson's order should include language stating that the fees are not reimbursed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Peterson (Trustee) in Propria Persona. Marta McNeill for Markee Quayle (Beneficiary). Peter Quayle in Propria Persona.

Mr. Quayle stated that the tools and the sculptures had been removed from the premises. Ms. McNeill reported that the fire inspection had been done, that it was determined that the lower unit was not legal and not rentable, and stated that if Peter Quayle remained in the unit, they may not be able to keep the fire insurance. In addition, she stated that the lower unit could be annexed into one of the other units. Mr. Peterson stated that only the storage spaces had been inspected, not the Peter's residence, that he was working with the insurance company on a number of items and that a re-inspection was going to be done. The Court stated that the inspection was to be done right away and continued the matter to December 16, 2008 for compliance.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Patrick Heard, CSR #11055. Clerk: R. Noguera/ J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy M. Mendoza.

Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called with case #291425 (The Phyllis D. Quayle Trust).

Appearances: Robert Peterson in Pro Per. Marta McNeill for and with Marky Quayle. Gregory O'Keeffe for Phylis Quayle (Conservatee/Beneficiary).

The Court informed Mr. Peterson that it would not allow the Trustees fees to be charged at the attorney rate of $250.00 per hour. After hearing oral argument, the Court took the issues regarding fees under submission. Ms. McNeill expressed concern about Peter Quayle residing in the property located on Broadway Street stating that his art work was being stored through out the premises, that the premises could be rented out to benefit the Conservatee, and proposed renting an apartment nearby so that Peter could be close to his Mother and continue to visit with her. The Court continued the issues regarding Peter Quayle to November 18, 2008. The Court ordered all art work be removed from the premises as well as the residence. If the property is not maintained and kept clear, the Court will consider Ms. McNeill's suggestion.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Linda Forster CSR#13286. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

Marky Quayle expressed willingness to loan money to the trust for the purpose of paying the outstanding debts. Mr. Siracusa indicated that the amount of money needed was more than what was being offered. The Court stated that it was obligated to see that the Conservatee was taken care of and granted the Petiton for Authority To Obtain A Reverse Mortgage Secured By The Trust's Real Property, (signing the order for the same in open court).
